key_metrics: Cost of Goods Sold: $10.81 billion, up 6.5% YoY
qoq_changes: Total sales and revenues contracted slightly by 0.7% YoY, driven by lower Machinery, Energy & Transportation sales.
Operating margins compressed significantly due to a $657 million increase in Cost of Goods Sold despite lower overall sales.
Share repurchases slowed to 2.67 million shares in Q2 2025 compared to 4.58 million shares in Q2 2024.
new_risks: Severe margin compression evident as Cost of Goods Sold outpaces revenue growth.
Potential demand softening indicated by the YoY decline in core Machinery, Energy & Transportation sales.
management_tone: Cautious; while the company continues to return capital to shareholders via dividends and buybacks, top-line stagnation and rising cost of goods sold are heavily pressuring operating margins and net profitability.
analyst_note: Caterpillar's Q2 2025 print reveals emerging profitability headwinds, with net income falling nearly 19% YoY due to a 6.5% rise in COGS against flat revenues. While the company maintained its strong capital return program, the core machinery segment's margin deterioration warrants close monitoring in upcoming quarters.
